data.title: Efforts to increase number of publicly traded companies face issues amid push for private market investments like Alphabet, Meta, Tesla.
data.description: For an extended period, policymakers have aimed to increase the quantity of publicly traded firms to offer everyday investors an opportunity to invest early in the next big companies like Alphabet, Meta, or Tesla.

data.title: Companies struggle with Bitcoin treasury strategy as share prices plummet.
data.description: As the value of Bitcoin drops, companies relying on it are seeing their stock prices plummet. This decline in value has hit many businesses hard, leading to significant losses in their treasuries. The sudden drop in Bitcoin's value has caused a ripple effect in the market and has raised concerns among investors. This situation underscores the volatility of cryptocurrencies and the risks associated with investing in them. The affected companies are now facing the challenge of recovering from these losses and finding ways to stabilize their financial positions.

data.title: Kenges Rakishev and Aselle Tasmagambetova's Shoqan School's One-Billion-Tenge Bond listed on KASE, a first for Kazakh schools.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global unemployment rate has risen to 6.7%, affecting approximately 198 million people worldwide. The highest increase was seen in low and middle-income countries, with the youth population being hit the hardest. The report also highlights the unequal impact on women, who are more likely to lose their jobs during the pandemic. Additionally, the data shows a significant decrease in working hours, equivalent to 255 million full-time jobs. These findings underscore the urgent need for targeted interventions to address the growing unemployment crisis on a global scale.

data.title: Scruggs Co. & NTS acquire MAV, a Kansas-based distributor in water sector, expanding reach. (Character count: 77)
data.description: On August 29, 2025, in Houston, Scruggs Company and Neil Technical Services (NTS), owned by Rox Capital Partners, disclosed their purchase of Mid-America Valve (MAV), a Kansas distributor focused on water and wastewater sectors. This acquisition marks a significant expansion...

data.title: Brazil's construction industry set to grow at 6.3% CAGR, reach $236B by 2034 due to government initiatives and infrastructure development.
data.description: A report on the Brazil construction market has been released, projecting significant growth from 2025 to 2034. In 2024, the market was valued at USD 127.63 billion and is expected to expand at a CAGR of 6.30%. By 2034, the market is predicted to reach USD 236 billion. The growth drivers include government projects, infrastructure development, and rising demand in construction sectors.

data.title: Nio (NIO) to release Q2 earnings with expected loss of 30 cents per share, revenue of $2.76 billion. Shares up 2.7% to $6.51.
data.description: NIO Inc. (NYSE: NIO) is set to disclose its second-quarter earnings before the market opens on Tuesday, September 2nd. Analysts are predicting the China-based company will post a loss of 30 cents per share for the quarter. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $2.76 billion based on information from Benzinga Pro. In July, NIO reported delivering a total of 21,017 vehicles, with 12,675 branded as Nio, 5,976 under the ONVO brand, and 2,366 from the FIREFLY brand. NIO shares saw a 2.7% increase, closing at $6.51 on Thursday. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Mercedes-Benz USA and Atlanta United's Brad Guzan support Furniture Bank of Metro Atlanta after fire with employee cookout, building furniture for local families in need.
data.description: Yesterday, Mercedes-Benz USA (MBUSA) organized a cookout for employees as a prelude to Labor Day. The gathering included staff and their families and was dedicated to fostering camaraderie and giving back to the community. One of the highlights was the furniture-building activity for the Furniture Bank of Metro Atlanta, aimed at assisting local families in need. The event aimed to aid the non-profit in restocking its inventory post a warehouse fire.

data.title: Online bans won't help smokers. Need more access to alternative products. U.S falling behind on smoke-free progress. Dr. Marina Murphy advocates for more choices and support for smokers.
data.description: In Washington, American smokers are urged to seek more options as an alternative to cigarettes in order to promote smoke-free progress. Dr. Marina Murphy, the Senior Director of Scientific Affairs at Haypp Group, emphasizes the need for increased choices, improved access, and a more supportive environment for individuals looking to switch to reduced-risk nicotine products. Dr. Murphy highlights that restrictive policies and slow regulations in the U.S. are hindering advancements in this area. She stresses the importance of expanding access to these products in her editorial titled "Why We Need Greater, Not Less, Access to Reduced-Risk Nicotine Products." It is vital to address this issue promptly as it impacts a significant number of smokers in the country.

data.title: BeOne Medicines sees stock rise after successful early trial for lymphoma patients with 125 participants.
data.description: Recently, BeOne Medicines Ltd. (NASDAQ: ONC) shared key findings from a Phase 1/2 clinical trial (BGB-11417-201) investigating the efficacy of sonrotoclax, a BCL2 inhibitor, in adult patients dealing with relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma (MCL) post-treatment with a Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor (BTKi) and anti-CD20 therapy. MCL is a less common aggressive subtype of B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) developing in lymph nodes' mantle zone. The study enrolled 125 participants, and the complete results are to be unveiled at an upcoming medical conference. BeOne is also leveraging Amgen's cancer drug royalties in a substantial pharma collaboration.
